tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows mild renal impairment (elevated creatinine), hypoglycemia, and low HbA1c. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), but no evidence of infection. Findings suggest possible diabetic nephropathy or another renal pathology, with poor glycemic control and possible dehydration or catabolic state. Clinical correlation and further evaluation are recommended.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
